framework.reasoning: No single ML framework covers this paper: it needs (a) PennyLane for exact statevector simulation of the 8-qubit encoding circuit (device='default.qubit', autograd/torch interface), (b) scikit-learn/XGBoost for the classical ridge/XGBoost benchmarks, and (c) a gradient-trained MLP/NN3 baseline. PyTorch is chosen as the schema-required 'primary' framework because it's the natural home for the MLP/NN3 baselines and because PennyLane's torch interface lets the same tensor stack flow through both the quantum circuit and any future gradient-based extensions; scikit-learn, XGBoost, and PennyLane are declared as first-class dependencies rather than folded into 'primary'. CUDA is not required -- n=8 qubits (256-dim statevector) and the classical models here are all comfortably CPU-tractable. Plain YAML suffices given the flat, enumerable hyperparameter grids (bandwidth/alpha/gamma) -- no nested sweep orchestration is needed beyond what a simple grid-search loop provides.

module_hierarchy.description: Builds and standardizes the 27 (main study) / 31 (diagnostic study) firm characteristics from raw exchange snapshots and quarterly financial statements, with announcement-date alignment (Sec 3.1, Table 1).

public_methods.name: standardize
public_methods.returns: cross-sectionally winsorized (+/-3 sigma) and standardized panel

module_hierarchy.description: Two universe constructions: point-in-time (constructible in real time) and static-screen (survivorship-conditioned) -- Sec 3.2.

public_methods.name: eligible_stocks
public_methods.returns: top-450 by trailing 20-day mean float mktcap, 252-day coverage>90%

public_methods.name: eligible_stocks
public_methods.returns: fixed top-450 by full-sample price coverage (2020-2025)

module_hierarchy.description: The twelve literature-catalogued pairwise interaction characteristics (Table 2), built as daily cross-sectional products of standardized components.
Per-window rank-IC screen: active set (|IC|>=0.015, min 6) and top-8 subset shared verbatim by all top-8 models (Sec 4.1).

public_methods.name: select
public_methods.signature: select(self, window_panel: pandas.DataFrame, window_returns: pandas.Series) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]

module_hierarchy.description: Bandwidth rescaling x~ = lambda*x, with the production {0.05,0.1,0.2,0.4} and widened 8-point [0.01,1.6] grids (Sec 4.2, Sec 8).

public_methods.name: scale
public_methods.signature: scale(self, x: numpy.ndarray, lam: float) -> numpy.ndarray
public_methods.returns: bandwidth-scaled inputs, clipped to [-3,3] before scaling

module_hierarchy.description: The IQP-style 8-qubit, R=2 ring-entangled encoding circuit (Eq. 1), built in PennyLane with exact statevector simulation.
Fidelity kernel |<psi(x)|psi(x')>|^2 and projected (Bloch-vector) kernel exp(-gamma*||phi(x)-phi(x')||^2) (Sec 4.2).

public_methods.name: compute_gram
public_methods.signature: compute_gram(self, X: numpy.ndarray, feature_map: QuantumFeatureMap) -> numpy.ndarray

public_methods.name: bloch_features
public_methods.signature: bloch_features(self, X: numpy.ndarray, feature_map: QuantumFeatureMap) -> numpy.ndarray

public_methods.name: compute_gram
public_methods.signature: compute_gram(self, phi: numpy.ndarray, gamma: float) -> numpy.ndarray

module_hierarchy.description: Classical RBF control kernel on the same top-8 inputs as the quantum branch (Sec 4.3, krr-rbf).

public_methods.name: compute_gram
public_methods.signature: compute_gram(self, X: numpy.ndarray, gamma: float) -> numpy.ndarray

module_hierarchy.description: Classical benchmark suite: ridge, XGBoost, MLP, NN3 ensemble, poly2ridge, ridge-int, xgb-x, qkrr-x rotation-pool variant, rank-average hybrid (Sec 4.4).

public_methods.name: fit_predict
public_methods.signature: fit_predict(self, X_train, y_train, X_test, alpha: float = 10.0) -> numpy.ndarray

public_methods.name: fit_predict
public_methods.returns: predictions on all pairwise products of top-8 features

module_hierarchy.description: Closed-form kernel ridge regression solve and the Nystrom full-budget extension (Sec 4.3, 5.1).

public_methods.name: fit
public_methods.signature: fit(self, K_train: numpy.ndarray, y_train: numpy.ndarray, alpha: float) -> numpy.ndarray

public_methods.name: predict
public_methods.signature: predict(self, K_test_train: numpy.ndarray, alpha_hat: numpy.ndarray) -> numpy.ndarray

public_methods.name: fit
public_methods.signature: fit(self, K_landmarks: numpy.ndarray, K_full_landmarks: numpy.ndarray, y_full: numpy.ndarray, alpha: float) -> numpy.ndarray
public_methods.returns: approximate full-budget dual coefficients via landmark extension

module_hierarchy.description: Walk-forward windowing engine: 252-day trailing training windows, 20-day rebalancing, 170 (main) / 60 (diagnostic) windows (Sec 4.1).

public_methods.name: iter_windows
public_methods.signature: iter_windows(self, panel_df: pandas.DataFrame, study: str) -> Iterator[dict]
public_methods.returns: yields per-window train/test slices and rebalance dates

module_hierarchy.description: IC/ICIR/t-stat/hit-rate/Sharpe/drawdown, paired t-test, Wilcoxon, and Holm family-wise correction (Sec 4.5, 5.2).

public_methods.name: rank_ic
public_methods.signature: rank_ic(self, predictions: numpy.ndarray, returns: numpy.ndarray) -> float
public_methods.returns: Spearman rank IC for one rebalance date

public_methods.name: paired_significance
public_methods.signature: paired_significance(self, ic_a: numpy.ndarray, ic_b: numpy.ndarray) -> dict

module_hierarchy.description: Regularized geometric difference g(Kc||Kq) diagnostic (Eq. 2, Sec 4.5).

public_methods.name: compute
public_methods.signature: compute(self, K_classical: numpy.ndarray, K_quantum: numpy.ndarray, lambda_g: float = 1e-6) -> float
public_methods.returns: scalar g, computed on a 400-point subset per window

tensor_flows.name: Quantum kernel-swap triplet, per window
steps: psi = QuantumFeatureMap.state(x_tilde) for each row -> [1536, 256] complex statevectors (n=8 qubits, R=2, ring entanglement)
K_rbf = ClassicalRBFKernel.compute_gram(x_tilde, gamma_rbf) -> [1536, 1536]  (same inputs, kernel-swap control)
alpha_hat = KernelRidgeRegression.fit(K_*, y, alpha) -> [1536]  (grid-searched alpha in {1e-3,1e-2,1e-1,1})

tensor_flows.name: Nystrom full-budget extension
steps: K_full_landmarks = FidelityKernel.compute_gram(psi_full, psi_landmarks) -> [~37800, 1536]  (cross Gram, landmarks vs. full window)

static_screen.screening_period: 2020-2025  # ASSUMED to condition on full 2020-2025 sample coverage, per Sec 3.2 text

quantum.hq_gate_interpretation: hadamard_conjugated_rz  # ASSUMED: SIR ambiguities[0], confidence 0.6 -- H_q undefined in paper
quantum.bandwidth_grid_widened: 8 points in [0.01, 1.6]  # exact 8 values not enumerated in paper beyond 0.01/0.2/0.4/0.8/1.6 mentioned in text
quantum.gamma_projected_kernel: tuned_per_window  # ASSUMED: SIR ambiguities[1], confidence 0.5 -- tuning procedure not stated

krr.subsample_stratification: by_date  # ASSUMED: SIR ambiguities[3], confidence 0.55 -- exact allocation scheme not detailed

classical_baselines.nn3_optimizer: adam  # ASSUMED: not stated in paper, confidence 0.5

runtime.reason: exact statevector simulation of the 8-qubit encoding circuit (explicitly cited by the paper, Bergholm et al. 2018)
Ridge, kernel utilities, train/val split helpers

Where to write per-window IC series and predictions

entrypoints.purpose: Compute pairwise significance (paired t, Wilcoxon), Holm-adjust across a test family, and reproduce Table 3/4/5-style comparison tables from saved per-window IC series.

cli_args.help: Directory of per-window IC series produced by run_study.py
Where to write the comparison table

entrypoints.purpose: Reproduce Section 8's bandwidth-grid widening and geometric-difference (Eq. 2) analysis, and Figure 2's two panels.

cli_args.help: Where to write geometry/bandwidth diagnostic outputs

risk_assessment.description: The H_q gate in the encoding circuit (Eq. 1) is undefined in the paper. If the implementation's interpretation (Hadamard-conjugated RZ) is wrong, the entire quantum feature map -- and every downstream fidelity/projected kernel result -- would not match the paper's actual circuit.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implemented as a swappable single-qubit gate function in QuantumFeatureMap so alternative interpretations (literal joint operator, separate Hadamard layer) can be substituted in one place; flagged prominently in code comments and README.

risk_assessment.description: The underlying data (Chinese A-share exchange snapshots, quarterly financial statements, 2010-2026) is proprietary/vendor-sourced and not publicly bundled or released by the author (paper states materials are 'available from the author on request', not a public repo/dataset).
risk_assessment.source: Paper's Data and code availability statement; no public dataset URL or DOI given
risk_assessment.mitigation: This repo implements the full methodology against a synthetic data generator (see data/synthetic.py) so the pipeline is runnable and testable end-to-end without the real dataset; real reproduction requires the user to source equivalent China A-share data independently (e.g. via a commercial vendor or exchange data feed) and conform it to the schema in data/README_data.md.

risk_assessment.description: Projected quantum kernel's bandwidth gamma has no stated tuning grid/procedure, unlike lambda and alpha.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Exposed as config.quantum.gamma_projected_kernel with a documented default tuning procedure (same validation-IC grid search as lambda); easy to swap for a fixed median-heuristic gamma if that later proves more faithful.

risk_assessment.description: The Nystrom extension's exact formula is not restated in this paper, only cited (Williams & Seeger 2001).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implemented as the standard Nystrom KRR approximation from the cited reference in NystromKRR; documented as an assumption in code and README.

risk_assessment.description: 'Stratified by date' subsampling to N=1536 has no detailed allocation scheme.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implemented as proportional-to-date random sampling in the walk-forward engine, exposed as config.krr.subsample_stratification for easy substitution.

risk_assessment.description: Simulating an 8-qubit statevector for N=1536 observations per window, across 170 (main) + 60 (diagnostic) windows, plus a widened bandwidth grid, is a nontrivial but classically tractable compute budget (no hardware required, but not instantaneous either).
risk_assessment.source: Architecture plan tensor_flows / Sec 9 ('no hardware, deliberately so')
risk_assessment.mitigation: QuantumFeatureMap batches statevector computation via PennyLane's broadcasting; a --debug flag runs a small synthetic smoke test (few windows, few observations) for fast iteration.

risk_assessment.description: MLP/NN3 baseline optimizer, learning rate, and epoch budget are not stated in the paper.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Adam, lr=1e-3, early-stopping on validation IC are used as reasonable defaults, exposed via config.classical_baselines.
